data.trigger: User joins the early-access waitlist before launch
data.goal: Keep waitlisters warm, build trust in the one-tap and estimate model, and prime the copy-paste onboarding prompt as a shareable growth loop.
data.voice_note: Calm, warm, confident, quietly witty. Sentence case. No hype, no false scarcity, no medical claims. Nutrition = estimates.
data.cadence: Email 1 immediately; 2 at +3 days; 3 at +8 days; 4 at +14 days; 5 on launch day.

emails.subject: You're on the list — here's what we're building
emails.preview: A month of meals, planned to your macros and budget.
emails.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Thanks for joining the Rasa early-access list. You're in.

Here's the promise, plainly: Rasa plans a month of meals around your macros and your budget, then orders them for you a tap at a time — starting with Swiggy. No daily 'what do I eat.' No blown budget. No falling off your diet by Wednesday.

And the line we won't cross: nothing gets ordered or spent without your tap.

We'll send you a few honest notes before launch — no hype, just what it is and how it works. Reply any time; a real person reads it.

Glad you're here,
The Rasa team

emails.subject: The part everyone asks about first: 'will it spend my money?'
emails.preview: Short answer: only when you tap yes.
emails.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

When we describe Rasa, the first question is almost always the same: is it going to order things behind my back?

No. Here's exactly how it works. Before every order, Rasa checks the restaurant, the price and whether the meal still fits your plan — then nudges you with the dish, the price and the arrival time. You tap yes, or you don't. If you don't, nothing is ordered and nothing is charged.

Allergens you flag are hard-blocked before anything reaches your cart. And there's a spend ledger you can open any time, showing planned vs actual against your cap.

Smart, but never behind your back. That's the whole design.

The Rasa team

emails.subject: Can restaurant macros actually be trusted?
emails.preview: Honest estimates, aggregated across the day.
emails.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Fair question — restaurant dishes don't come with a nutrition label. So here's how Rasa handles macros, honestly.

We ground each dish in an India-native food database, estimate a sensible portion, and target your macros across the whole day rather than obsessing over any single dish. Small per-dish errors tend to cancel out, so the daily total lands roughly within 10-15%.

We label every figure as an estimate, because that's what it is. Rasa is about everyday balance — not medical claims, not precision you can't actually get from a menu.

We'd rather be honest than impressive.

The Rasa team

emails.subject: A prompt you can try before we even launch
emails.preview: Run your own meal-preference interview in ChatGPT or Claude.
emails.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Want a taste of how onboarding will feel? Here's a shareable Rasa prompt you can paste into your own ChatGPT or Claude today. It interviews you about your diet, goals, budget and dislikes, then hands you a tidy profile.

When Rasa launches, you'll paste that profile straight in and skip setup. Until then, it's a genuinely useful way to think through how you eat — and it's fun to share with the friend who also lives on delivery.

[Get the prompt]

Not long now.
The Rasa team

emails.subject: Rasa is live — your month is waiting
emails.preview: Set it up once. Tap yes at mealtimes.
emails.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

It's here. As an early-access member, you're first in.

Set up your profile (or paste in the one you made with the prompt), approve your 30-day plan, and start tapping yes at mealtimes. Your routine meals, planned to your macros and your cap, ordered a tap at a time through Swiggy.

[Start with Rasa]

Thank you for waiting with us. Now go stop deciding lunch.

The Rasa team

P.S. One tap per order. Allergens hard-blocked. Nutrition figures are estimates.
